policy_document.title: Global Employee and Operational Policies Handbook

policy.title: General Code of Conduct
policy.summary: This policy sets the foundational behaviors expected of all AuraTech workforce members, including full-time employees, part-time staff, and external contractors.
policy.section: All personnel must perform their duties in a professional and ethical manner. We maintain a zero-tolerance policy for harassment, discrimination, or intimidation. For any inquiries, please contact our general workplace support team at support@auratech.com or call 555-019-8800.
Contractors and temporary staff are issued a blue badge. Office access is limited to standard business hours (8:00 AM to 6:00 PM local time) unless pre-authorized by a project manager.

policy.title: Hybrid Work and PTO Allowance
policy.summary: Defines hybrid work schedules, remote setup allowances, and Paid Time Off (PTO) tracking for full-time employees.
policy.section: Full-time employees are eligible for a hybrid working arrangement. Employees are expected to work from an AuraTech corporate office at least 2 days per week (typically Tuesdays and Thursdays). For remote work setups, employees receive a one-time stipend of $500.

Standard full-time employees accrue 15 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) annually, accrued monthly at a rate of 1.25 days. All PTO requests must be submitted through the Employee Portal at least 5 business days in advance.
For critical HR emergencies or leave queries, contact the HR Benefits Specialist, Sarah Jenkins, at sarah.jenkins@auratech.com or call her desk phone at 555-014-9922.

policy.title: Performance Management and Compensation Adjustments
policy.summary: Procedures and policies governing annual performance appraisals, promotion triggers, and discretionary salary adjustments.
policy.section: Managers must complete annual performance appraisals for all direct reports by November 30th. Appraisals are graded on a scale of 1 (Needs Development) to 5 (Outstanding). Reviews are stored securely in the HR system.

Discretionary salary adjustments must be approved by the Department VP and HR Director, Marcus Sterling (marcus.sterling@auratech.com, office line: 555-011-4477). Discretionary salary increases are capped at 8% annually. Promotions require a minimum of 12 months in the current role and an appraisal grade of 4 or higher.

If a direct report is underperforming, the manager must implement a formal 30-day Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) in consultation with the Employee Relations Lead, David Ross (david.ross@auratech.com).
